images.caption: Figure 1:Visual Memory Mechanisms.Unlike Standard LVLMs that degrade viavisual dilutionor Injection methods that causeserial interference, ourPersistentVisualMemory (PVM) establishes an independent retrieval path, preserving visual intensity without disrupting the autoregressive flow.

Figure 2:Power-Law Decay of Visual Signal.Log-scale analysis confirms thatΩ𝒱\Omega_{\mathcal{V}}tightly follows the𝒪​(t−1)\mathcal{O}(t^{-1})trajectory predicted by Theorem3.1.
This demonstrates that visual attention mass is structurally diluted, decaying inversely to the sequence lengthtt.

Figure 2:Power-Law Decay of Visual Signal.Log-scale analysis confirms thatΩ𝒱\Omega_{\mathcal{V}}tightly follows the𝒪​(t−1)\mathcal{O}(t^{-1})trajectory predicted by Theorem3.1.
This demonstrates that visual attention mass is structurally diluted, decaying inversely to the sequence lengthtt.

Figure 3:Evolution of Textual Dominance.The TVR trajectory effectively validates the proposed two-phase dilution mechanism.
This reveals how initial probability competition saturates into a strict equilibrium, where textual priors structurally overwhelm visual signals.

Figure 4:Overview of the Persistent Visual Memory (PVM) framework.PVM is integrated parallel to the frozen FFN to shield active visual retrieval from sequential dilution during autoregressive reasoning. It treats the hidden state as a query to retrieve specific visual contexts via a parameter-efficient bottleneck adapter (consisting of Projection, Cross-Attn, FFN, and Restoration). The module employs a Silencing Mask to activate selectively during text generation, injecting the retrieved visual signal into the main stream through a learnable gate to enhance visual grounding.

Table 1:Main Results on General and Reasoning Benchmarks.We report 4-run average accuracy (%) across 8 benchmarks, with the best and second-best performances highlighted inboldandunderlined. The numbers in subscripts denote the absolute improvement (↑\uparrow) or decline (↓\downarrow) relative tothe original model baseline.PVM (SFT + GRPO)achieves the best overall performance, demonstrating consistent gains in both comprehensive understanding and specialized reasoning.

Figure 5:Performance Gain vs. Token Length.The relative improvement scales with sequence length, surging to +27.3% in the “Long” group. This confirms PVM helps structurally mitigate visual signal dilution in deep generation.

Figure 5:Performance Gain vs. Token Length.The relative improvement scales with sequence length, surging to +27.3% in the “Long” group. This confirms PVM helps structurally mitigate visual signal dilution in deep generation.
Figure 6:Layer-wise Prediction Convergence.The steeper descent and distinct gap (shaded) confirm that PVM accelerates prediction convergence compared to strong baselines.

images.caption:
Figure 7:Detailed Spatiotemporal Decay of Visual Attention.The heatmap illustrates the evolution of visual attention massΩ𝒱\Omega_{\mathcal{V}}across all 36 layers of Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct. The x-axis represents the number of generated text tokens, and the y-axis represents the layer index.
Darker regions indicate lower visual attention.
A distinct decay forms in the intermediate layers as the sequence grows, highlighting the structural necessity for the Persistent Visual Memory (PVM) module.
